<!-- generated: 2026-05-29 / uoink v3.1 -->
# Uoink full site context

## URL: https://uoink.video/

Uoink turns videos and podcasts into private local corpora for Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and MCP-capable agents. One click under a video captures transcript, screenshots, comments, channel context, and metadata. The corpus lands on disk and on the clipboard. The local helper exposes 13 MCP tools for agents.

Core position: Uoink that shit. Transcript-only tools flatten video into text. Uoink keeps the source shape: words, frames, comments, channel context, metadata, and optional Hook Type classification.

## URL: https://uoink.video/install

Install the desktop helper, install the browser extension, then click the Uoink button. The Windows helper runs from %LOCALAPPDATA%\Uoink and exposes localhost 127.0.0.1:5179. Browser extension support targets Chromium browsers. Chrome Web Store approval is pending, so the release zip is the launch path. macOS is on the v3.1 track.

## URL: https://uoink.video/how

The workflow is click -> corpus -> AI -> result. Uoink captures timestamped transcript, screenshots, comments, metadata, channel context, and classifier blocks. Paste into Claude or ChatGPT, or let an MCP agent call Uoink directly. Captures accumulate into a local library that can be searched and cited later.

## URL: https://uoink.video/features

Capabilities include in-page Uoink button, right-click capture, playlist mode, universal URL mode, podcast RSS feed support, local SQLite FTS5 search, topic folders, Memory dashboard, entity mentions, Hook Type taxonomy, BYO Anthropic key, clipboard budget, Whisper model selector, local MCP server, and Operator Skill instructions. Uoink is a local asset builder, not only a transcript downloader.

## URL: https://uoink.video/podcasts

Podcast support adds RSS feed polling, local audio download, local Whisper transcription, and speaker diarization. Diarization labels speakers as Speaker 1, Speaker 2, and so on until the user renames them. Podcast corpora live beside video corpora and are searchable by agents.

## URL: https://uoink.video/agents

Uoink is an MCP server. Supported clients include Claude Desktop, Cursor, Cline, Continue, and ChatGPT Desktop where MCP stdio is supported. Stdio is the supported transport. HTTP JSON-RPC at 127.0.0.1:5179/mcp is experimental. The setup page generates the real local config path after install.

MCP tools: uoink_video, uoink_playlist, get_job_status, cancel_job, list_recent_uoinks, search_uoinks, get_uoink_corpus, analyze_comments, classify_hook, get_taxonomy, get_citation_map, get_uoink_health, find_mentions.

## URL: https://uoink.video/mcp

The MCP page and /mcp/manifest.json expose server metadata, supported transports, install links, client config notes, and tool names in machine-readable form for agents.

## URL: https://uoink.video/privacy

Uoink has no account, no Uoink cloud, no telemetry, and no hosted corpus. Captures live on disk. Network calls go to the source being extracted and, only when optional AI features are enabled, Anthropic with the user's own API key. The key is stored in the operating system credential vault.

## URL: https://uoink.video/changelog

The changelog covers v3.1 direction, v2.2 dashboard and tray work, MCP tools, and in-flight macOS, podcast, markdown memory, and verification assistance.

## URL: https://uoink.video/terms

Uoink is MIT-licensed local software. Users are responsible for how they use captured materials and for following source platform and AI provider terms.
